folio.label: 190v
folio.l: Do thilke carte aresten , boldly
My gold caused my mordre , sotú to seyn
And told hi~ euäy poynt , how he was slayn
Witú a ful pytous face , pale of hewe
And trostetú wel , this dreem he fond ful trewe
For oon the morwe , as sone as it was day
To his felawes In , he took his way
And whan æ he cam , to this Oxes stalle
After his felawe , he bygan to calle
The hostiler answered hi~ , a noon
And seide sire , ©oure felawe is a gon
As sone as day , he went out of the Toun
This man gan fallen , in suspecioun
Remembryngö on hise dreemes , æ he mette
And fortú he gootú , no lengere wold he lette
Vn to the west gate of the toun , and fond
A dongö cart , went for to donge lond
That was arrayed , in the same wyse
As ©e han herd , the dede man deuyse
And witú an hardy herte , he gan to crie
Vengiau~ce and iustice , of this felonye
My felawe murdred is , this same nygút
And in this Carte he litú , gapyngö vp rigút
I crie out on the Ministres , quod he
That shulden kepe & reule , this Citee
Harrow allas , heere litú my felawe slayn
What shulde I more , vn to this tale seyn
The peeple out stirte , & cast the cart to the grounde
And in the myddel of the donge , they founde
The dede man , æ murdrid was al newe
¢ O blisful god , æ art so iust and trewe
Loo how æat æè bywreyest , murdre alway
Murdre wol out , that se we day by day
Murdre is so wlatsom , & abhomynable
To god , that is so iust and resonable
That he ne wol nat suffre it , heled be
Thougú it abyde , a ©er or two or three
Murdre wol out , this is my co~clusioun~
And rigút a noon , Mynistres of the toun
Han hent the Carter , and so sore hi~ pyned
And eke the hosteler , so sore engyned
That æei be knew , here wykkednesse a noon
And were an hangid , by the nekke boon
